author: Susan "Lady D" Damon

applications.old_text: Matthew 18:11 has textual-placement differences across Bible editions. The production use treats the assigned reference as a rescue-focused saying for planning purposes, with final wording and placement to be confirmed during translation and permissions review.
applications.new_text: Matthew 18:11 has textual-placement differences across Bible editions. Treat this day as rescue-focused while final wording and placement are confirmed during translation and permissions review.

repeated_sentences.phrase: Let the Spirit press this from information into formation.
repeated_sentences.action: Rewrite Volume 3 formation bridges so the Spirit language feels alive and not template-driven.

repeated_sentences.phrase: Do not rush past the verse; let it steady you before you move.
repeated_sentences.action: Vary the intake line with fresh morning verbs: sit, receive, notice, breathe, confess, carry.

repeated_sentences.phrase: Let prayer turn insight into obedience before noon.
repeated_sentences.action: Keep obedience-as-response, but write more specific today-sized applications.

repeated_sentences.phrase: Take one surrendered step before worry gets the first word.
repeated_sentences.action: Replace repeated surrender line with concrete steps tied to each day's passage.

repeated_sentences.phrase: Start from what God has revealed, not from what the day is demanding.
repeated_sentences.action: Vary the opening claim so it sounds like Lady D speaking into that specific morning.

repeated_sentences.phrase: This day falls on Saturday, the seventh-day Sabbath in the 2026 production calendar.
repeated_sentences.action: Keep the Saturday Sabbath truth, but move repeated production-calendar wording into a reusable Sabbath note or vary it by entry.

repeated_sentences.phrase: Walk out of this page with courage, tenderness, and clarity.
repeated_sentences.action: Review in the author-voice pass and vary where repetition sounds mechanical rather than intentional.

judge_loops.standard: Sounds like Lady D sitting with the reader in the morning: warm, reverent, direct, practical, not generic or academic.
Saturday Sabbath is protected, obedience is response to grace, and Father/Son/Spirit lanes stay distinct without separating God.

judge_loops.pass_count: 3 passes per volume after every rewrite wave
judge_loops.standard: No mechanical openings, repeated prayers, recycled morning-impact lines, or repeated bridge sentences unless deliberately liturgical.
References-only remains safe until a final Bible translation and copyright notice are locked.
Markdown, DOCX, PDF, ZIP, public download links, and Vercel pages all match the same current evidence.

gpt_settings.recommended: Best available flagship writing/reasoning model, medium reasoning
gpt_settings.use_for: Rewriting 7 to 14 entries at a time while preserving Scripture reference, day title, prayer, journal prompt, and Adventist guardrails.

gpt_settings.recommended: Best available flagship model, extra-high reasoning
gpt_settings.use_for: Theology, voice, repetition, permissions, and KDP readiness audits. Use three passes for routine batches and up to seven for difficult theological or voice sections.
Link checks, count verification, HTML index updates, artifact inventories, and non-theological formatting checks.

gpt_settings.recommended: GPT image workflow with the strongest available image model and high-quality portrait/book-cover settings
gpt_settings.use_for: 6 x 9 cover variants, typography-free concept art, full-wrap direction boards, and visual comparison sheets.

release_boundary: This package is an author-voice copyedit gate, not a final KDP upload approval.
The reader-facing lens labels are clean across the 1,098 source entries and public mirrors.
The next manuscript work is a line-level voice and rhythm pass, followed by Bible permissions, final interiors, cover regeneration from locked page counts, KDP Previewer, and physical proof review.
